relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The word returns: send my people to serve/worship.
Refusal brings frogs as creation overflowing boundaries.
The Nile's swarm invades the spaces of power and daily life.
No rank is untouched by the sign.
Aaron stretches the staff over waters.
Frogs cover the land.
The magicians imitate but cannot heal.
Pharaoh asks for relief and promises release.
Moses lets Pharaoh name the time of intercession.
Tomorrow becomes a witness that none is like YHWH.
The frogs will return to their proper place.
Moses cries to YHWH concerning the frogs.
YHWH answers, and the frogs die from houses and fields.
The land smells of the false promise's aftermath.
Relief becomes Pharaoh's excuse to harden again.
Dust itself becomes a sign.
The dust-swarm touches human and beast.
The magicians cannot reproduce this sign.
They name the finger of Divine, but Pharaoh still refuses.
Moses meets Pharaoh at the water with the same demand.
The swarm threatens the houses of Egypt.
Goshen is set apart as protected space.
A distinction is placed between oppressed people and oppressor system.
The swarm ruins the land of refusal.
Pharaoh offers worship without real freedom.
Moses refuses a compromised worship that creates danger.
The wilderness journey must be as YHWH says.
Pharaoh bargains: go, but not far.
Moses names the pattern of deceit.
Moses pleads to YHWH again.
The swarm is removed completely.
Pharaoh makes his heart heavy again and refuses release.

contemplative_note: Exodus 8 exposes the cycle of empire: crisis, bargaining, relief, and renewed refusal. Liberation cannot be managed by Pharaoh's conditions.
